title: The One With All The Poker

lines.text: The whole gang is helping Rachel mail out resumes while whistling the theme from The Bridge on the River Kwai.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Rach, we're running low on resumes over here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you really want a job with Popular Mechanics?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, if you're gonna work for mechanics, those are the ones to work for.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, look, you guys, I'm going for anything here, OK? I cannot be a waitress anymore, I mean it. I'm sick of the lousy tips, I'm sick of being called 'Excuse me...'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, did you proofread these?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh... yeah, why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, nothing, I'm sure they'll be impressed with your excellent compuper skills.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (upset) Oh my Goood! Oh, do you think it's on all of them?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no, I'm sure the Xerox machine caught a few.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Ross and Chandler are sitting at a table. Rachel is working. Monica and Phoebe enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey... hi, ladies... uh, can I get you anything? (to Monica, quietly): Did you bring the mail?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Lots of responses.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica): Really? (out loud): Sure, we have scones left! (to Monica): OK, read them to me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading): Dear Ms. Green, thank you for your inquiry, however... oh... (crumples up letter)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (out loud): We have apple cinnamon...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (reading): OK... Dear Ms. Green... yeah... yeah... yeah... No. (crumpes up letter)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading): Your Visa bill is huge!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (grabs the bill) Give me that!
Camera cuts to Chandler and Ross at table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I can't believe you. Linda is so great! Why won't you go out with her again?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is this still about her whole 'The Flintstones could've really happened' thing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's not just that. It's just-I want someone who... who does something for me, y'know? Who gets my heart pounding, who... who makes me, uh... (begins to stare lovingly at Rachel)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...little playthings with yarn?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could you want her more?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcastically) Dee, the sarcastic sister from Whats Happening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I am totally, totally over her, OK, I just... (Rachel comes over, Ross lays head on table): Hiiii!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! How are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We're fine, we're fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK. (walks away)
Ross keeps staring at her, head on table. Chandler smacks him with a newspaper. Joey enters, Ross and Chandler laugh at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're not-we're not saying anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhhhh... Joey cried last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the girls) We were playing poker, alright...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There was chocolate on the three. It looked like an eight, alright?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, guys, you should've seen him. 'Read 'em and weep.'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And then he did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, now, how come you guys have never played poker with us?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, what is that? Like, some kind of guy thing? Like, some kind of sexist guy thing? Like it's poker, so only guys can play?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, women are welcome to play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, OK, so then what is it? Some kind of... you know, like, like... some kind of, y'know, like... alright, what is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There just don't happen to be any women in our games.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, we just don't happen to know any women that know how to play poker.

lines.speaker: Girls
lines.text: Oh, yeah, right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, please, that is such a lame excuse!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, that's a typical guy response.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me, do any of you know how to play?

lines.speaker: Girls
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But you could teach us.

lines.speaker: Guys
lines.text: No.
Monica and Rachel's, the guys are teaching the girls how to play poker.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (teaching) OK, so now we draw cards.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I wouldn't need any, right? Cause I have a straight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good for you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Congratulations!
Microwave timer goes off. Monica gets up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK Phoebs, how many do you want?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, I just need two... the, um, ten of spades and the six of clubs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. No, uh, Phoebs? You can't-you can't do...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait, I have the ten of spades! Here! (gives it to Phoebe)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. Uh... no, see, uh, you-you can't do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no-no-no-no, that's OK, I don't need them. I'm going for fours.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you're... (gives up)
Monica comes back to the table with plates of food.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, here we go. We've got salmon roulettes and assorted crudites.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: OOooooo!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, Monica, what're you doin'? This is a poker game. You can't serve food with more than one syllable. It's gotta be like chips, or dip, or pretz...(look of realization)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (changing subject) OK, so at this point, the dealer...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, you know, we got it, we got it. Let's play for real. High stakes... big bucks...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, now, you sure? Phoebe just threw away two jacks because they didn't look happy...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But... I'm ready, so, just deal.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK, alright, last minute lesson, last minute lesson. (holds up two cards) Joey... three... eight. Eight... three. (Joey is unamused) Alright babe, deal the cards.
Time lapse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (throws down her cards) Dammit, dammit, dammit!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey): Oh I see, so then, you were lying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: About how good your cards were.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Heh... I was bluffing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A-ha! And... what is bluffing? Is it not another word for... lying?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, sorry to break up this party, but I've got resumes to fax before work tomorrow... (gets up to leave)

lines.speaker: Guys
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rach, Rach, we gotta settle.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Settle what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The... Jamestown colony of Virginia. You see, King George is giving us the land, so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The game, Rachel, the game. You owe us money for the game.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what, you guys? It's their first time, why don't we just forget about the money, alright?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hell no, we'll pay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, Monica? I had another answer all ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And you know what? We want a rematch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well that's fine with me. Could use the money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross): So basically, you get your ya-yas by taking money from all of your friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pause)...Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and I get my ya-yas from Ikea. You have to put them together yourself, but they cost a little less.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, Rachel, this is poker. I play to win, alright? In order for me to win, other people have to lose. So if you're gonna play poker with me, don't expect me to be a 'nice guy,' OK? Cause once those cards are dealt... (claps hands three times)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pause)...Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm not a nice guy.
Ross' apartment. Chandler and Joey are there. Ross enters with a pizza.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright boys, let's eat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, did you get that from the 'I Love Rachel' pizzeria?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You still on that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on. What was with that whole Black Bart speech? (mimicking): "When I play poker, I'm not a nice guy!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You are way off, pal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I don't think so, see Ross, because I think you love her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um.... no. See, I might've had feelings for her at one time-not any more. I just-I...
Marcel makes a screeching noise in background.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel! Where are you going with that disc?
Marcel puts a CD in the player.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You are not putting that on again! Marcel, OK-if you press that button, you are in very, very big trouble.
The Lion Sleeps Tonight starts to play. Marcel starts to dance.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe are there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (opening mail) Can you believe what a jerk Ross was being?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I know. He can get really competitive.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ha. Ha, ha.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hello, kettle? This is Monica. You're black.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please! I am not as bad as Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I beg to differ. The Pictionary incident?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was not an incident! I-I was gesturing, a-and the plate slipped out of my hand.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oooooh. (reads letter) (surprised): Oh! I got an interview! I got an interview!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're kidding! Where? Where?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (in disbelief): Sak's... Fifth... Avenue.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, Rachel!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's like the mother ship is calling you home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what's the job?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Assistant buyer. Oh! I would be shopping... for a living!
Knock on door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK, look. That is Aunt Iris. This woman has been playing poker since she was five. You gotta listen to every word she says. (opens door) Hi!

lines.speaker: Aunt Iris
lines.text: Is Tony Randall dead?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Aunt Iris
lines.text: Well, he may be now, because I think I hit him with my car.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Aunt Iris
lines.text: No! That's bluffing. Lesson number one. (walks into kitchen) Let me tell you something... everything you hear at a poker game is pure crap. (to Phoebe): Nice earrings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank y... (thinks about it)

lines.speaker: Aunt Iris
lines.text: Girls, sit down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, Aunt Iris? This is Phoebe, and that's Rachel...

lines.speaker: Aunt Iris
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, listen, I am parked at a meter. Let's do it.
Ross's apartment, everyone but Rachel is seated around his table. The Lion Sleep Tonight plays in the background.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross, could we please, please, please listen to anything else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright.
Ross shuts off the CD player. Marcel runs into the bedroom and slams the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna pay for that tonight.
Knock on door. Ross opens it. Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Guys! Guess what, guess what, guess what, guess what!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Um, ok... the... the fifth dentist caved and now they're all recommending Trident?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noooo... the interview! She loved me! She absolutely loved me. We talked for like two and a half hours, we have the same taste in clothes, and-oh, I went to camp with her cousin... And, oh, the job is perfect. I can do this. I can do this well!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: That's great! That's wonderful!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, oh, and then she told the funniest story...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK, great. You'll tell us and we'll laugh. Let's play poker.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright now listen, you guys, we talked about it, and if you don't want to play, we completely understand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes, yes, we could play some other game... like, uh, I don't know... Pictionary?
The guys all duck under the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ha, ha, very funny, very funny. But I think we'd like to give poker another try. Shall we, ladies?

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Yes, we should. I think we should.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Rach, do you want me to shuffle those?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, thats OK. Y'know, I think I'm gonna give it a go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright... (shuffles cards expertly, all the guys stare in amazement)
Commercial Break
Ross's Apartment, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, Phoebs owes $7.50, Monica, you owe $10, and Rachel, you owe fifteen big ones.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But hey, thanks for teachin' us Cross-Eyed Mary. You guys, we gotta play that at our regular game.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, here's my $7.50. (Hands them the money) But I think you should know that this money is cursed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I cursed it. So now bad things will happen to he who spends it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's alright, I'll take it. Bad things happen to me anyway. This way I can break 'em up with a movie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, that just leaves the big Green poker machine, who owes fifteen...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mmm-hmmm. Oh, so typical. Ooo, I'm a man. Ooo, I have a penis. Ooo, I have to win money to exert my power over women. (hands over her money)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what? This is not over. We will play you again, and we will win, and you will lose, and you will beg, and we will laugh, and we will take every last dime you have, and you will hate yourselves forever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm. Kinda stepped on my point there, Mon.
Monica and Rachel's, everyone is there ready for another poker game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, you gals wanna hand over your money now? That way, we don't have to go through the formality of actually playing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooooh, that's fine. We'll see who has the last laugh there, monkey boy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK, we done with the chit-chat? Are we ready to play some serious poker?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (holding a card and waving it in front of her face) Hey you guys, look, the one-eyed jack follows me wherever I go. (they look at her) Right, OK, serious poker.
Ross gets up from the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse me, where are you going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh... to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you want to go to the bathroom, or do you wanna play poker?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I want to go to the bathroom. (exits)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, well, I'm gonna order a pizza. (gets up)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no-no, I'm still waiting to hear from that job and the store closes at nine, so you can eat then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's fine. I'll just have a Tic-Tac to hold me over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, Cincinnati, no blinds, everybody ante. (deals cards)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looks at her cards) Yes! (everyone looks at her) .... or no.
Ross comes back from bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright. (to Rachel): Your money's mine, Green.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Your fly is open, Geller. (he checks it, and zips up)
Time lapse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, you know what I just realized? 'Joker' is 'poker' with a 'J.' Coincidence?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, that's... that's 'joincidence' with a 'C'!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh... Phoebe? Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Um... I'm out. (throws in cards)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too. Alright, whattaya got.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you better hop outta the shower, cause... I gotta flush. (lays down cards)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, well, well, hop back in bucko, cause I got four sixes! (lays down cards) I won! I actually won! Oh my God! Y'know what? (collects chips) I think I'm gonna make a little Ross pile. (holds up a chip) I think that one was Ross's, and I think-oh-that one was Ross's. Yes! (Starts singing): Well, I have got your money, and you'll never see it...
Ross stands up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And your fly's still open...
Ross looks down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ha, I made you look....
Time lapse.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I couldn't be inner. Monica?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica, in or out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (slams down cards) I hate this game!
Joey slides a plate away from Monica towards Chandler, who hides it under the table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK Joey, your bet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhh, I fold like a cheap hooker who got hit in the stomach by a fat guy with sores on his face. (the girls look at him, confused) Oh, I'm out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I am very in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Couldn't be more out. (throws in cards)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too. Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, I will see you... and I'll raise you. (throws chips in pot) What do you say... want to waste another buck?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, not this time. (he folds) So... what'd you have?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not telling. (collects chips)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, show them to me. (reaches for her cards, Rachel covers them up)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No..!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Show them to me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Get your hands out of there! No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me see! Show them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I've had dates like this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (deals new hand) Boy, you really can't stand to lose, can you? Your whole face is getting red... little veins popping out on your temple...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Plus that shirt doesn't really match those pants.
Ross is visibly upset.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: First of all, I'm not losing...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you are losing. Definitely losing. (phone rings)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let's not talk about losing. Just deal the...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (answering phone) Hel-lo, Rachel Green.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (mimicking Rachel) Mee mee, mee-mee mee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Excuse me. (covers up phone; to Ross) It's about the job.
Rachel walks into kitchen to talk on the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Barbara! Hi, how are you? (Listens) Uh-huh. (Listens) No, I understand. Yeah. Oh, oh, come on, no, I'm fine. Don't be silly. Yeah... oh, but you know, if-if anything else opens up, plea-Hello? Hello? (hangs up phone, very depressed)
Rachel goes back and sits down. The rest don't know what to say.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sorry, Rach.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, there's gonna be lots of other stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah...(sigh)....OK. Where were we? Oh, OK... five card draw, uh... jacks or better... nothing wild, everybody ante.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Rachel, we don't have to do this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, we do. (pause)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, check.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm in for fifty cents. (throws it in)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Call.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I see your fifty cents... and I raise you... five dollars. (throws it in)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I thought, uh... it was a fifty cent limit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I just lost a job, and I'd like to raise the bet five bucks. Does anybody have a problem with that?
Everyone says no and folds, except for Ross, who thinks about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross): Loser?
Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Phoebe back their chairs away from the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I fold. (lays cards down, and gets up)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you mean, you fold? Hey, come on! What is this? I thought that 'once the cards were dealt, I'm not a nice guy.' I mean, what, were you just full of it?
Ross thinks it over, finally sits down and picks up his cards.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm in. (throws in chips)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How many you want?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One. (Rachel gives him the card.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Dealer takes two. (she deals herself two cards) What do you bet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I bet two dollars. (throws it in)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK... see your two... and I raise you twenty. (throws it in)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I see your twenty, raise you twenty-five. (throws it in)
The other four look amazed at the large pot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See your twenty-five...and...uh, Monica, get my purse.
Monica gets up, looks in Rachel's purse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, there's nothing in it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, then get me your purse.
Monica gets Rachel her purse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK, here you go. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica): Thank you. (to Ross): I saw your twenty-five, and I raise you... seven.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ...teen! (throws in a ten-dollar bill)
Ross looks in his wallet, pulls out two dollars.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey): Joey, I'm a little shy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's OK, Ross, you can ask me. What?
Ross looks at Joey, dumbfounded at his stupidity.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross): What do you need, what do you need?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fifteen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, here's ten. (gives it to him)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here, I got five, I got five. (Ross takes the money)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel): OK, I am calling your seventeen. What do you got?
Long pause as they both look at each other.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (lays down cards) Full house.
Ross stares at her. Thinks about it. Puts cards on table, face down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You got me.
Monica and Phoebe get up and start celebrating in the kitchen, pouring wine and singing. Rachel, shocked, goes to join them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross): Ahhh, that's alright. Y'know, that's a tough hand to beat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross): I thought we had them!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well, when you don't have the cards, you don't have the cards, you know. (looks at Rachel) But, uh... look how happy she is. (smiles)
Chandler and Joey look at her, and then look back at him. They dive for Ross's hand to see what he had, and he tries to stop them from looking.
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, all six are playing Pictionary at Monica's apartment. Monica is drawing a picture, and the three guys are guessing. She draws what looks like an airplane.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Airplane! Airport! Airport '75! Airport '77! Airport '79!
Timer goes off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, time's up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (pointing at the drawing, upset) Bye... bye... BIRDIE.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's a bird?
Monica glares at Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's a bird!
Monica sits, Rachel gets up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, OK, it's my turn. (reads the answer)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Go.
Rachel starts drawing what looks like a bean.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh.... bean! Bean!
Rachel begins tapping the picture of the bean frantically.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (triumphantly) The Unbearable Likeness of Being!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That, you get? That, you get?
Monica picks up a glass to take a drink, everyone ducks as though she was about to throw it.
End
